name: Sort Then Scan
summary: Sort the array so consecutive values are adjacent, then count runs in one linear pass.
visualizationIntent: init: the raw array is shown, then .sort() reorders the cells into ascending order (a real reflow step); loop(i): pointer i scans left to right, cell i-1 is compared against cell i; check(duplicate): equal neighbors are skipped; update(consecutive): when nums[i] == nums[i-1]+1 the current run grows; update(reset): a gap resets the run to 1; the running best shows in the readout; return: the sorted array rests and LONGEST RUN holds the answer.

lineExplanations.3: If the array is empty there is no sequence to measure.
lineExplanations.5: Sort the array so consecutive values sit next to each other.
lineExplanations.6: The current run starts at length 1.
lineExplanations.7: The best run starts at length 1.
lineExplanations.8: Scan from the second element to the end.
lineExplanations.9: If this value equals the previous one, it is a duplicate.
lineExplanations.10: Skip the duplicate without changing the run.
lineExplanations.11: If this value is exactly one more than the previous, the run continues.
lineExplanations.12: Grow the current run by one.
lineExplanations.14: Otherwise a gap broke the run, so reset it to 1.
lineExplanations.15: Update the best run length seen so far.
lineExplanations.16: Return the length of the longest consecutive run.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class the platform runs.
syntaxExplanations.2: def longestConsecutive(self, nums): the method receiving the numbers.
syntaxExplanations.3: if not nums: is True when the list is empty.
syntaxExplanations.5: nums.sort() sorts the list in place, ascending.
syntaxExplanations.6: current_count = 1 initializes the running run length.
syntaxExplanations.7: max_count = 1 initializes the best run length.
syntaxExplanations.8: for i in range(1, len(nums)) scans indices 1..n-1.
syntaxExplanations.9: nums[i] == nums[i - 1] tests whether this value repeats the previous.
syntaxExplanations.10: continue skips to the next index.
syntaxExplanations.11: nums[i] == nums[i - 1] + 1 tests whether the values are consecutive.
syntaxExplanations.13: else: introduces the fallback branch for a gap.
syntaxExplanations.15: max(max_count, current_count) keeps whichever is larger.
syntaxExplanations.16: return max_count hands back the answer.
